meta.title: Toolbar
meta.description: Toolbars organize related actions and controls in a compact bar for easy access. They provide consistent placement for common or contextual tasks within an application.

mdx:
## Overview

A toolbar is a handy container that organizes a group of related buttons and
tools, using both horizontal and vertical layouts to fit the screen;
essentially, it gives users quick access to the most common actions they need
right now.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=5733-23105&m=dev)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Visual options

#### Horizontal

Most commonly used, horizontal toolbars should sit above the content meant to be
altered.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar orientation="horizontal">
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.MoreHoriz />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatListBulleted />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatListNumbered />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

#### Vertical

Vertical toolbars are less common, but in the right interface it can be more
helpful than a horizontal placement.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar orientation="vertical">
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
      <Icons.FormatBold />
    </IconButton>
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
      <Icons.FormatItalic />
    </IconButton>
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
      <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
    </IconButton>
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
      <Icons.MoreHoriz />
    </IconButton>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

#### Plain and outline

Two versions are available, a plain version that has a slight shadow to lift,
and an outlined version that has more contrast from elements around it. The
plain version without an outline is most common.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" gap="600" alignItems="center">
    <Toolbar variant="plain" size="xs">
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.MoreHoriz />
      </IconButton>
    </Toolbar>

    <Toolbar variant="outline" size="xs">
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.MoreHoriz />
      </IconButton>
    </Toolbar>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Groups

Grouping similar tools is a common best practice within all toolbars. Please
combine similar tools in groups to make it easier for users to make choices
quickly. If there are a lot of tools within a group, fold them under a menu
button and leave 2-3 out that are the most popular choices.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar size="xs">
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.MoreHoriz />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatListBulleted />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatListNumbered />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

A toolbar is a handy container that organizes a group of related buttons and
tools, using both horizontal and vertical layouts to fit the screen;
essentially, it gives users quick access to the most common actions they need
right now.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=5733-23105&m=dev)


a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar orientation="horizontal">
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.MoreHoriz />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatListBulleted />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost">
        <Icons.FormatListNumbered />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Keyboard Navigation:** Ensure all interactive elements within the toolbar
  are navigable using the tab key in a logical order, following the order of
  items.
- **ARIA Roles:** Apply the ARIA role toolbar to the container element.
- **Labeling:** Provide descriptive, concise aria-labels for the toolbar
  container and for all icon buttons to ensure screen reader users understand
  the component's purpose and the action it performs.
- **Provide clear status:**
  - Use the disabled state for actions that are temporarily unavailable. Do not
    hide actions that are simply disabled, as this can cause shifting layouts
    and confuse users.
  - Clearly communicate the reason an action is disabled via tooltips or other
    contextual help.

dev.mdx:
## Getting started

### Import

```tsx
import { Toolbar, type ToolbarProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus';
```

### Basic usage

The simplest implementation creates a horizontal toolbar with default styling:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar>
    <Button variant="ghost">File</Button>
    <Button variant="ghost">Edit</Button>
    <Button variant="ghost">View</Button>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

## Usage examples

### Size options

The `xs` and `md` size variants are available to match your interface density:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text textStyle="sm" mb="200" color="neutral.11">Size: xs</Text>
      <Toolbar size="xs">
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="New">
          <Icons.Add />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Save">
          <Icons.Save />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Print">
          <Icons.Print />
        </IconButton>
      </Toolbar>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text textStyle="sm" mb="200" color="neutral.11">Size: md</Text>
      <Toolbar size="md">
        <IconButton size="md" variant="ghost" aria-label="New">
          <Icons.Add />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="md" variant="ghost" aria-label="Save">
          <Icons.Save />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="md" variant="ghost" aria-label="Print">
          <Icons.Print />
        </IconButton>
      </Toolbar>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Visual variants

Choose between `plain` and `outline` variants to match your design context:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text textStyle="sm" mb="200" color="neutral.11">Variant: plain</Text>
      <Toolbar variant="plain">
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Undo">
          <Icons.Undo />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Redo">
          <Icons.Redo />
        </IconButton>
      </Toolbar>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text textStyle="sm" mb="200" color="neutral.11">Variant: outline</Text>
      <Toolbar variant="outline">
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Undo">
          <Icons.Undo />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Redo">
          <Icons.Redo />
        </IconButton>
      </Toolbar>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Orientation

The `orientation` prop controls the layout direction and keyboard navigation:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="600" alignItems="flex-start">
    <Box>
      <Text textStyle="sm" mb="200" color="neutral.11">Horizontal (default)</Text>
      <Toolbar orientation="horizontal">
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Home">
          <Icons.Home />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Search">
          <Icons.Search />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Settings">
          <Icons.Settings />
        </IconButton>
      </Toolbar>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text textStyle="sm" mb="200" color="neutral.11">Vertical</Text>
      <Toolbar orientation="vertical">
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Home">
          <Icons.Home />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Search">
          <Icons.Search />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Settings">
          <Icons.Settings />
        </IconButton>
      </Toolbar>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

**Behavioral differences:**
- `orientation="horizontal"`: Arrow Left/Right keys navigate between controls
- `orientation="vertical"`: Arrow Up/Down keys navigate between controls

### With Groups and Separators

Organize toolbar controls using `Group` and `Separator` components:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar>
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="New">
        <Icons.Add />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Open">
        <Icons.FolderOpen />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Save">
        <Icons.Save />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Print">
        <Icons.Print />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Undo">
        <Icons.Undo />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Redo">
        <Icons.Redo />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

### With ToggleButtonGroup

Integrate toggle button groups for stateful toolbar controls:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar variant="outline">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Root
      selectionMode="multiple"
      defaultSelectedKeys={["bold"]}
    >
      <IconToggleButton
        id="bold"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Bold"
      >
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="italic"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Italic"
      >
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="underline"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Underline"
      >
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconToggleButton>
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Root
      selectionMode="single"
      defaultSelectedKeys={["left"]}
    >
      <IconToggleButton
        id="left"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Align Left"
      >
        <Icons.FormatAlignLeft />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="center"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Align Center"
      >
        <Icons.FormatAlignCenter />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="right"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Align Right"
      >
        <Icons.FormatAlignRight />
      </IconToggleButton>
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

## Component requirements

## Accessibility

The Toolbar component handles most accessibility requirements internally, including:
- Setting the `role="toolbar"` attribute
- Managing keyboard navigation with arrow keys
- Setting proper `aria-orientation` based on the `orientation` prop

However, you should always provide an accessible label for the toolbar. This can be done by:

- Using `aria-label` for a direct label:

```tsx
<Toolbar aria-label="Text formatting controls">
  {/* toolbar controls */}
</Toolbar>
```

- Using `aria-labelledby` to reference a visible label:

```tsx
<Text id="toolbar-label">Text Formatting</Text>
<Toolbar aria-labelledby="toolbar-label">
  {/* toolbar controls */}
</Toolbar>
```

If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component:

```tsx
const PERSISTENT_ID = "example-toolbar";

export const Example = () => (
  <Toolbar id={PERSISTENT_ID} aria-label="Main toolbar">
    {/* toolbar controls */}
  </Toolbar>
);
```

#### Keyboard navigation

The component supports full keyboard interaction:
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab`: Move focus into and out of the toolbar
- `Arrow Left` / `Arrow Right`: Navigate between controls in horizontal toolbars
- `Arrow Up` / `Arrow Down`: Navigate between controls in vertical toolbars
- `Home`: Focus first control in the toolbar
- `End`: Focus last control in the toolbar

## API reference

<PropsTable id="Toolbar" />

## Common patterns

### Text editor toolbar

Create a comprehensive text formatting toolbar with multiple control groups:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => {
  const [textStyle, setTextStyle] = useState("p");

  const textStyles = [
    { id: "h1", label: "Heading 1" },
    { id: "h2", label: "Heading 2" },
    { id: "p", label: "Paragraph" },
  ];

  const selectedTextStyle = textStyles.find((v) => v.id === textStyle);

  return (
    <Toolbar size="md" variant="outline" aria-label="Text formatting">
      <Group>
        <Menu.Root onAction={(v) => setTextStyle(String(v))}>
          <Menu.Trigger
            borderRadius="200"
            border="1px solid"
            borderColor="neutral.6"
            width="3500"
            aria-label="Text style"
          >
            <Box display="flex" alignItems="center" gap="200" px="200">
              <Text>{selectedTextStyle?.label}</Text>
              <Icons.KeyboardArrowDown />
            </Box>
          </Menu.Trigger>
          <Menu.Content>
            {textStyles.map((style) => (
              <Menu.Item key={style.id} id={style.id}>
                {style.label}
              </Menu.Item>
            ))}
          </Menu.Content>
        </Menu.Root>
      </Group>
      <Separator orientation="vertical" />
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Root
        selectionMode="multiple"
        defaultSelectedKeys={["bold"]}
        aria-label="Text formatting"
      >
        <IconToggleButton id="bold" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Bold">
          <Icons.FormatBold />
        </IconToggleButton>
        <IconToggleButton id="italic" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Italic">
          <Icons.FormatItalic />
        </IconToggleButton>
        <IconToggleButton id="underline" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Underline">
          <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
        </IconToggleButton>
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
      <Separator orientation="vertical" />
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Root
        selectionMode="single"
        defaultSelectedKeys={["left"]}
        aria-label="Text alignment"
      >
        <IconToggleButton id="left" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Align Left">
          <Icons.FormatAlignLeft />
        </IconToggleButton>
        <IconToggleButton id="center" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Align Center">
          <Icons.FormatAlignCenter />
        </IconToggleButton>
        <IconToggleButton id="right" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Align Right">
          <Icons.FormatAlignRight />
        </IconToggleButton>
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
    </Toolbar>
  );
}
```

### Navigation toolbar

Build a vertical navigation toolbar for sidebar layouts:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar
    orientation="vertical"
    variant="outline"
    aria-label="Navigation"
  >
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Home">
      <Icons.Home />
    </IconButton>
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Search">
      <Icons.Search />
    </IconButton>
    <Separator orientation="horizontal" />
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Settings">
      <Icons.Settings />
    </IconButton>
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Profile">
      <Icons.Person />
    </IconButton>
    <Separator orientation="horizontal" />
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Logout">
      <Icons.Logout />
    </IconButton>
  </Toolbar>
)
```

### Action toolbar

Create a simple action toolbar with primary controls:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400" width="full">
    <Toolbar aria-label="Document actions">
      <Group>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="New document">
          <Icons.Add />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Open document">
          <Icons.FolderOpen />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Save document">
          <Icons.Save />
        </IconButton>
      </Group>
      <Separator orientation="vertical" />
      <Group>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Undo">
          <Icons.Undo />
        </IconButton>
        <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Redo" isDisabled>
          <Icons.Redo />
        </IconButton>
      </Group>
    </Toolbar>
    <Box
      p="600"
      borderRadius="200"
      borderWidth="1px"
      borderColor="neutral.6"
      minHeight="6000"
    >
      <Text color="neutral.11">Document content area</Text>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

## Testing your implementation

These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using Toolbar within your application. As the component's internal functionality is already tested by Nimbus, these patterns help you verify your integration and application-specific logic.

### Basic Rendering Tests

Verify the component renders with expected elements

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import { Toolbar, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Toolbar - Basic rendering", () => {
  it("renders toolbar with role attribute", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar aria-label="Main toolbar">
          <Button size="xs">File</Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    // Verify toolbar is rendered with correct role
    expect(
      screen.getByRole("toolbar", { name: /main toolbar/i })
    ).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders with horizontal orientation by default", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar aria-label="Test toolbar">
          <Button size="xs">File</Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    const toolbar = screen.getByRole("toolbar");
    expect(toolbar).toHaveAttribute("aria-orientation", "horizontal");
  });

  it("renders with vertical orientation when specified", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar orientation="vertical" aria-label="Test toolbar">
          <Button size="xs">File</Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    const toolbar = screen.getByRole("toolbar");
    expect(toolbar).toHaveAttribute("aria-orientation", "vertical");
  });
});
```

### Keyboard Navigation Tests

Test keyboard navigation with the toolbar

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import { Toolbar, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Toolbar - Keyboard navigation", () => {
  it("navigates between controls with arrow keys in horizontal toolbar", async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar aria-label="Test toolbar">
          <Button size="xs" data-testid="btn-1">
            File
          </Button>
          <Button size="xs" data-testid="btn-2">
            Edit
          </Button>
          <Button size="xs" data-testid="btn-3">
            View
          </Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    const btn1 = screen.getByTestId("btn-1");
    const btn2 = screen.getByTestId("btn-2");
    const btn3 = screen.getByTestId("btn-3");

    // Focus first button
    await user.click(btn1);
    expect(btn1).toHaveFocus();

    // Navigate with Arrow Right
    await user.keyboard("{ArrowRight}");
    expect(btn2).toHaveFocus();

    await user.keyboard("{ArrowRight}");
    expect(btn3).toHaveFocus();

    // Navigate backwards with Arrow Left
    await user.keyboard("{ArrowLeft}");
    expect(btn2).toHaveFocus();
  });

  it("navigates between controls with arrow keys in vertical toolbar", async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar orientation="vertical" aria-label="Test toolbar">
          <Button size="xs" data-testid="btn-1">
            Home
          </Button>
          <Button size="xs" data-testid="btn-2">
            Search
          </Button>
          <Button size="xs" data-testid="btn-3">
            Settings
          </Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    const btn1 = screen.getByTestId("btn-1");
    const btn2 = screen.getByTestId("btn-2");
    const btn3 = screen.getByTestId("btn-3");

    // Focus first button
    await user.click(btn1);
    expect(btn1).toHaveFocus();

    // Navigate with Arrow Down
    await user.keyboard("{ArrowDown}");
    expect(btn2).toHaveFocus();

    await user.keyboard("{ArrowDown}");
    expect(btn3).toHaveFocus();

    // Navigate backwards with Arrow Up
    await user.keyboard("{ArrowUp}");
    expect(btn2).toHaveFocus();
  });
});
```

### Testing Toolbar Children

Test that toolbar properly contains and manages child elements

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import { Toolbar, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Toolbar - Children", () => {
  it("renders multiple child buttons", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar aria-label="Test toolbar">
          <Button size="xs">File</Button>
          <Button size="xs">Edit</Button>
          <Button size="xs">View</Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /file/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /edit/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /view/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("maintains button functionality within toolbar", async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    const handleClick = vi.fn();

    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Toolbar aria-label="Test toolbar">
          <Button size="xs" onClick={handleClick} data-testid="clickable-btn">
            Click me
          </Button>
        </Toolbar>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    const button = screen.getByTestId("clickable-btn");
    await user.click(button);

    // Verify the click handler was called (may be called multiple times due to event bubbling)
    expect(handleClick).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });
});
```


## Resources

- [Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-toolbar--docs)
- [React Aria Toolbar](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Toolbar.html)
- [ARIA Toolbar Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

These guidelines provide comprehensive direction for designing and developing
toolbars within Nimbus.

### Best practices

- **Contextual placement:** Place the toolbar directly above the content it
  controls (e.g., above a text editor, above a data table). This ensures an
  immediate, clear connection between the tools and the content being modified.
- **Logical grouping:** Group related actions visually using small separators
  (like vertical lines or subtle spacing) to aid readability. For example, group
  text alignment controls together, and export actions together.
- **Hierarchical order:** Arrange items based on usage frequency and logical
  flow, placing the most common or primary actions prominently on the left (in
  LTR languages).
- **Minimize clutter:** Avoid overcrowding the toolbar. If there are too many
  items, use secondary controls like overflow menus to hide less frequently used
  actions.

### Usage

A toolbar's use is to provide a dedicated, contextual area for a set of related,
frequently used actions and controls to manage the content directly below it.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Group related actions and controls** in a compact, container.
- **Offer contextual actions** relevant to the content being viewed or edited
  (e.g., formatting options in a text editor, or bulk actions for a list or
  table).
- **Maintain screen space** by consolidating many items into a single, compact
  bar.

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **The primary purpose is navigation** between different pages or application
  views; use a navigation component or tabs instead.
- **Actions are rarely used or require complex input;** use a menu or modal
  dialog to house these actions to simplify.
- **The area needs to display a large amount of primary content** without being
  obscured.
- **Only one or two actions are needed** for a view; consider placing these
  directly or within a relevant component, like a card.

### Toolbar in use

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Do group related actions and controls in a compact container.
> - Offer contextual actions relevant to the content being viewed or edited
>   (e.g., formatting options in a text editor, or bulk actions for a list).
> - Maintain screen space by consolidating many items into a single, compact
>   bar.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Toolbar aria-label="Text formatting">
    <Select.Root
      size="sm"
      defaultSelectedKey="paragraph"
      aria-label="Text style"
      isClearable={false}
    >
      <Select.Options>
        <Select.Option id="heading1">Heading 1</Select.Option>
        <Select.Option id="heading2">Heading 2</Select.Option>
        <Select.Option id="paragraph">Paragraph</Select.Option>
      </Select.Options>
    </Select.Root>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Root
      selectionMode="multiple"
      defaultSelectedKeys={["bold"]}
    >
      <IconToggleButton id="bold" size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Bold">
        <Icons.FormatBold />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="italic"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Italic"
      >
        <Icons.FormatItalic />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="underline"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Underline"
      >
        <Icons.FormatUnderlined />
      </IconToggleButton>
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
    <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="More formatting options">
      <Icons.MoreHoriz />
    </IconButton>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Root selectionMode="single" defaultSelectedKeys={[]}>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="bulleted"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Bulleted list"
      >
        <Icons.FormatListBulleted />
      </IconToggleButton>
      <IconToggleButton
        id="numbered"
        size="xs"
        variant="ghost"
        aria-label="Numbered list"
      >
        <Icons.FormatListNumbered />
      </IconToggleButton>
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
    <Separator orientation="vertical" />
    <Group>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Undo" isDisabled>
        <Icons.Undo />
      </IconButton>
      <IconButton size="xs" variant="ghost" aria-label="Redo">
        <Icons.Redo />
      </IconButton>
    </Group>
  </Toolbar>
);
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't overcrowd the toolbar with too many items; use an overflow menu
>   instead.
> - Don't place unrelated actions next to each other without visual separation
>   to group similar functions.
> - Don't use a standard text button with a label when an icon button is
>   sufficient and space is limited.
> - Don't assume the user understands an icon without a descriptive tooltip.
> - Don't hide actions that are temporarily disabled, as this causes shifting
>   elements and breaks muscle memory.
